title: WHATWG-Informed HTML Trees, Cheerio, parse5, and Parser Differentials · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for whatwg-informed html trees, cheerio, parse5, and parser differentials and accepts only compiler plus runtime changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named network, DNS, browser, accessibility, security, load, legal-review, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for Cheerio tree-construction evidence adapter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed…
objectives: Distinguish source tokens, parse5-repaired tree, Cheerio selections, browser DOM, accessibility tree, and rendered state.
Choose document, fragment, HTML, or XML loading options deliberately and test wrapper insertion and case behavior.
Test misnesting, omitted elements, tables, templates, foreign content, duplicate attributes, and foster parenting.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Tree Budget
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript team handles case 4697 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove Cheerio loading modes, malformed tree repair, parser differentials, budgets, and source-tree distinctions; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound source, nodes, depth, attributes, text, selector work, and parse duration and expose rejection causes.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Tree Budget.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for bound source, nodes, depth, attributes, text, selector work, and parse duration and expose rejection causes.
